title: Backup approval without full authority
prompt: A finance exception process partially fails because the primary senior approver is unavailable. The team proposes routing all approvals through backup staff so work can continue. This fallback appears workable, but the backup staff do not hold authority for the highest-risk exception tier. The original objective is continuous and valid approval handling, not merely keeping tickets moving. Provide the revised recovery recommendation.

title: Rollback without data integrity
prompt: A system release partially fails and the team proposes rollback. User access can be visually restored quickly, so the rollback path looks sufficient on the surface. However, the rollback restores interface behavior only and leaves several transaction-state records inconsistent underneath. The original objective is controlled restoration of operational integrity, not just surface recovery. Provide the revised rollback judgment.

title: Manual reporting without sustained capacity
prompt: An automated reporting pipeline partially fails, and the operations team proposes a manual spreadsheet process as a fallback. The manual route can cover the next report cycle, so it appears to solve the problem. But volume analysis shows that the manual process cannot sustain daily operational load beyond a short window. The original objective is dependable reporting continuity, not one-cycle appearance of recovery. Provide the revised recovery plan.

title: Alternate vendor without full compliance
prompt: A supply chain disruption affects a required hardware component. Procurement proposes an alternate vendor so the program can recover schedule quickly. The substitute component seems operationally compatible, but later review shows it fails one mandatory compliance requirement. The original objective is valid and deployable recovery, not simply restoring timeline confidence. Provide the revised supplier recovery recommendation.

title: Emergency queue without endurance
prompt: A support operation partially fails after a staffing disruption. Managers propose routing cases into an emergency queue staffed by a reduced team. The fallback keeps visible service moving for the first two days, so it appears acceptable. But service quality deteriorates sharply after 48 hours and unresolved complexity begins to accumulate. The original objective is sustainable service continuity, not short-lived queue motion. Provide the revised recovery design.

title: Temporary access without audit trail
prompt: A system access outage partially disrupts operations. An administrator proposes a temporary access workaround so users can continue their work. The workaround restores entry successfully, so it appears to be a valid recovery path. However, the temporary path bypasses normal logging and breaks the audit trail for privileged actions. The original objective is restored access with intact governance, not access alone. Provide the revised recovery recommendation.

title: Partial communication recovery
prompt: A primary communication system partially fails during a policy rollout. The team switches to a secondary messaging channel, and early signs suggest the message is getting through. Later review shows the backup channel reaches only part of the full stakeholder set, leaving several critical recipients uncovered. The original objective is effective policy communication to the full required set, not partial message continuity. Provide the revised communication recovery plan.

title: Incident recovery without root repair
prompt: An operational incident is resolved through a quick recovery workflow, and visible service resumes. The team treats this as successful recovery because customers can use the system again. However, the root dependency that triggered the incident remains unchanged and unstable. The original objective is durable recovery, not merely temporary symptom clearance. Provide the revised recovery assessment.
